title: Largest 1-Bordered Square
description: Given a 2D grid of 0s and 1s, return the number of elements in the largest square subgrid that has all 1s on its border, or 0 if such a subgrid doesn't exist in the grid.

solutionCode.python:
```python
def prev_perm_opt1(arr):
    n = len(arr)
    for i in range(n - 2, -1, -1):
        if arr[i] > arr[i + 1]:
            j = i + 1
            while j < n and arr[j] < arr[i]:
                j += 1
            arr[i], arr[j - 1] = arr[j - 1], arr[i]
            return arr
    return arr
```
    
    - Starting from the right end of the array, search for the first pair of elements where the left one is greater than the right one.
- Then, find the next greatest element on the right side which is smaller than the left element.
- Swap these two elements to create the lexicographically largest permutation that is smaller than the original array.
- If the array is already the lexicographically smallest permutation, return the same array.
